spark.observation: /qor-substantiate (and adjacent lifecycle skills) bakes in assumptions that the host repo is a Python project with a specific release ceremony shape. Hit on Qor-logic Customer-App-3.0 (React 18 + bun + Supabase) on 2026-05-06 during a security migration session -- 8 of ~15 substantiate steps either failed loudly or silently no-op'd; session ended in SUBSTANTIATE DEFERRED rather than a seal.
spark.initial_question: How does Qor-logic deliver its governance value proposition (Merkle-sealed audit trail) on non-Python host repos without requiring each consumer to re-implement the lifecycle from scratch?
spark.why_now: Recurring: the issue is from 2026-05-06; the same shape will hit every non-Python consumer until the substantiate step bodies honor archetype detection that /qor-bootstrap + /qor-organize already perform. Qor-logic's CONCEPT claims general SDLC applicability; this gap contradicts the stated scope.

affected_actors: Non-Python repo operators running Qor-logic (TypeScript, Rust, Go, polyglot)
Qor-logic maintainers (every cross-language consumer that fails silently is a future SG entry)
Downstream auditors / compliance reviewers reading sealed META_LEDGER entries
problem_frame.failure_mode: When /qor-substantiate runs against a non-Python repo, the seal entry can claim chain completeness while 5+ gate steps silently skipped. The Merkle hash anchors a half-checked state. Operators either decline to seal (governance trail breaks) or hand-author seal (skill protocol bypass).
problem_frame.cost_of_failure: The governance trail is the value proposition. A seal that lies about gate coverage is worse than no seal -- it manufactures false confidence and contaminates the chain for any downstream consumer trusting it. Cross-workspace federation (GH #51) would amplify the contamination.

transformation_statement: Non-Python repo operators move from substantiate fails mid-flight or silently skips half the gates, leaving the ledger contaminated with a half-checked seal to substantiate runs to completion with explicit per-step skipped/ran status, and the seal entry records which gates ran versus were skipped due to host-archetype prerequisites without rewriting the lifecycle from scratch per language, weakening the Python-host ceremony, or producing seals that overclaim coverage.

non_goals: Full multi-language re-implementation of every gate (no porting qor.scripts.secret_scanner to JS/Rust/Go in V1)
New release-ceremony framework (we honor existing ceremonies, not replace them)
limitations: V1 lands the skill capability declaration mechanism (Option 1; smallest viable surface). Pluggable backends (Option 2) and two-track skill split (Option 3) are V2/V3.
Skipped gates surface in seal entry + Process Shadow Genome events; they do NOT block seal. Operator judgment decides whether half-coverage is acceptable for their compliance posture.
exclusions: No changes to first-party Qor-logic-on-Qor-logic seal ceremony (must remain backward-compatible -- current Python toolkit path is one valid archetype).
No new gate steps in this phase -- only capability declarations on existing steps.
forbidden_interpretations: This is NOT permission to silently skip security gates. When prerequisites are absent the seal entry MUST surface the gap; skipped: prerequisite absent is itself an explicit signal, not a no-op.
This is NOT a green light to author seals without a META_LEDGER entry. Only the per-gate completeness narrative inside the entry becomes archetype-aware.

options_matrix.name: Skill capability declaration
options_matrix.summary: Each substantiate step declares requires: (e.g., requires: pyproject.toml, requires: qor.scripts.secret_scanner). At skill-load, missing-requirement steps log skipped: prerequisite absent + emit a Process Shadow Genome event. Seal entry surfaces which gates ran vs skipped.
options_matrix.selection_reason: Smallest reversible step. Would have unblocked the 2026-05-06 session. Honors existing archetype detection without forcing language ports. Forward-compatible with options 2 + 3.

options_matrix.name: Pluggable backends
options_matrix.summary: version_bump, changelog_stamp, release_artifact_compile each get a backend selector chosen by .qor/workspace.json archetype (already produced by /qor-organize).
options_matrix.rejection_reason: Larger surface; each backend is its own integration project. Defer to V2 once V1 capability declarations show which backends matter most via Process Shadow Genome usage data.

options_matrix.name: Two-track skill split
options_matrix.summary: Split into qor-substantiate-core (language-agnostic gates) + qor-substantiate-release (version/changelog/tag/dist ceremony). Core always runs; release runs only when matching backend is declared.
options_matrix.rejection_reason: Structurally clean but disruptive -- breaks every existing skill citation to /qor-substantiate. Premature without first knowing which backend points the field actually demands (signal from Option 2 usage).

evidence_required: Tests proving each declared requires: actually controls skip vs run
Tests proving a seal entry with skipped gates surfaces the skip list verbatim in the SESSION SEAL body
Tests proving Python-host first-party seals still produce the same coverage as today (no regression in Qor-logic-on-Qor-logic)
Process Shadow Genome event schema extension gate_skipped_prerequisite_absent with severity-1 default
Doctrine entry SG-HalfSealedClaim-A cataloguing the seal claims coverage it does not have pattern with the 2026-05-06 originating recurrence
escalation_triggers: If implementation finds that any gate currently lacks a clean prerequisite signal (i.e., the gate's prerequisite is intertwined with the gate's behavior), escalate to /qor-refactor before continuing.
If the seal-entry surfacing format would silently look identical to all gates ran in any rendering (terminal, ledger viewer, federation consumer), escalate to /qor-audit before merge.

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Prerequisite check misclassifies a present prerequisite as absent (false-negative)
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Test in regression suite asserts Python-host first-party seal still runs all 15 gates; CI catches if any gate flips to skipped on the canonical host
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Revert capability declaration to no-op; re-audit the gate's prerequisite predicate

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Prerequisite check misclassifies an absent prerequisite as present (false-positive -- gate runs and crashes)
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Existing ABORT paths in each gate surface as test failure on synthetic non-Python fixture
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Tighten the predicate; add a fixture covering the missed-shape

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Seal entry surfaces skip list but downstream federation consumer treats skipped == ran
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Cross-workspace ledger replay finds two seals with same gate-coverage claim where one is half-skipped
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Pause federation reconciliation; emit gate_skipped_prerequisite_absent events to alert

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Capability declaration mechanism itself drifts (e.g., requires: syntax changes silently)
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Lint at /qor-audit Step 0.6 detects malformed or undeclared requirements on any modified substantiate step
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: VETO audit; route to /qor-plan to amend

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Operator routinely overrides skipped warnings without justification (governance-norm decay)
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Override-friction escalator already exists (Phase 54 wiring); session-total mode (Phase 69) catches the recurrence
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Surface to /qor-remediate; consider raising the gate's severity from advisory to blocking

readiness.rationale: Problem frame is concrete (2026-05-06 incident), options matrix is decided (Option 1 V1; Options 2+3 explicitly deferred), scope is bounded (capability declarations only; no language ports; no new gates), evidence required is enumerable. Research is not needed -- we already know which gates exist, which prerequisites each requires, and where the archetype detection lives (/qor-bootstrap, /qor-organize).
